The user wants a short, polite two-paragraph email to opposing counsel proposing to extend a contract-review deadline by one week. They explicitly said generic wording is fine and not to reference any specific agreement. They just need the email text.

Subject: Request for One-Week Extension on Contract Review

Dear [Counsel],

I hope this message finds you well. I'm writing to respectfully request a short extension on the current contract review deadline — specifically, an additional seven (7) business days. This would move the target completion date to [new date]. The additional time would allow us to complete our review thoroughly and minimize the need for further back-and-forth on substantive points.

Please let me know if this works on your end, or if you'd like to discuss a different arrangement. Thank you for your understanding and flexibility, and I look forward to wrapping this up promptly.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
